compiled_state.title: As an agent, I can use a create-bug skill and guidance to produce well-formatted bug reports
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Done Definitions

- /dso:create-bug skill exists referencing the bug report template and correct ticket command
- Skill does not add --tags CLI_user by default (only the calling agent knows invocation context)
- Skill uses .claude/scripts/dso shim (not direct plugin paths) to avoid shim enforcement blocks
- Using-dso guidance instructs agents to use the template when creating bugs (inlined in skill if using-dso doc not yet available per epic dso-khl2)
- Skill is a reference doc (guidance), not a behavioral skill — no eval scaffold required

## Scope

In: Lightweight SKILL.md for /dso:create-bug, using-dso guidance text.
Out: Template creation (Story 1), CLI validation (Story 2), test creation (Story 4).

## Considerations

- check-shim-refs.sh will block commits with direct plugin script references — use .claude/scripts/dso shim throughout
- Soft dependency on epic dso-khl2 for using-dso doc; inline guidance as fallback

## Escalation Policy

Autonomous — Agents proceed with best judgment at all times. Reasonable assumptions are made and documented. No escalation for uncertainty.

comments.body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /dso:create-bug skill file exists at plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md
- [ ] Skill references bug-report-template.md
  Verify: grep -q 'bug-report-template' plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md
- [ ] Skill uses .claude/scripts/dso shim (not direct plugin paths)
  Verify: grep -q '.claude/scripts/dso' plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md
- [ ] Skill does not add --tags CLI_user by default
  Verify: { grep -q 'CLI_user' plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md && grep -q 'do not\|does not\|should not\|only.*calling agent' plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md; } || ! grep -q 'CLI_user' plugins/dso/skills/create-bug/SKILL.md; true
comments.author: Main
